articles.title: IRGC Rains Fire On US Ally; F-35 Jet Hangars Attacked; 'Crushing & Decisive Operation’
articles.description: Iran claims it has launched coordinated attacks against multiple U.S. military targets across the region. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) says it targeted the U.S. al-Azraq Air Base in Jordan, the Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait, and the headquarters of the U.S. Fifth Fleet in Bahrain in response to recent American strikes. The attacks come after President Donald Trump accused Iran of bringing down a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, a claim strongly denied by Tehran. Jordan says it intercepted missiles aimed at al-Azraq, while U.S. officials say the vast majority of Iranian missiles and drones were shot down before reaching their targets.#iran #usa #usiran #iranvsus #middleeast #irgc #donaldtrump #jordan #kuwait #bahrain #fifthfleet #straitofhormuz #usmilitary #missileattack #droneattack #middleeastcrisis #irannews #usnews

articles.title: Israeli PM Netanyahu to run for re-election after Trump raises doubts
articles.description: Benjamin Netanyahu will seek re-election this year, his party announced on Wednesday, after U.S. President Donald Trump said he wasn't sure if the Israeli prime minister would stand again.

articles.title: Sushmita Dev Quits TMC & Rajya Sabha, Delivering Fresh Blow To Mamata Banerjee As TMC Crisis Deepens
articles.description: Senior Trinamool Congress leader Sushmita Dev has resigned from the party & Rajya Sabha, marking the second resignation by a TMC MP in recent days after Sukhendu Sekhar Roy's exit. The development comes amid growing turmoil within Mamata Banerjee's party following its defeat in the West Bengal Assembly elections. Dev's recent meeting with Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has further fuelled speculation about her political future, even as the party grapples with rebellion from MLAs, dissent among MPs and a widening leadership crisis.

articles.title: Why a city in Lebanon could decide whether the Iran-Israel war ends or starts again
articles.description: Iran says any ceasefire must include Lebanon. Israel says Lebanon is a separate battlefield. That disagreement is becoming the biggest threat to fragile efforts to prevent a wider regional war.

articles.title: TMC MP Sushmita Dev's resignation accepted by Rajya Sabha Chairman C P Radhakrishnan from Upper House, TMC faces another setback
RS Chairman C P Radhakrishnan accepts TMC MP Sushmita Dev's resignation
articles.description: Rajya Sabha Chairman C P Radhakrishnan accepted TMC MP Sushmita Dev's resignation. This marks another setback for Mamata Banerjee's TMC. Dev cited a long story for her decision. Her exit follows Sukhendu Sekhar Ray's resignation. The party faces internal turmoil and speculation of a split. Dev joined TMC in 2021 after leaving Congress.

articles.title: F-35 fighter shelters, US bases in Bahrain, Jordan & Kuwait hit by Iran as Mojtaba begins revenge
articles.description: The U.S.-Iran conflict has intensified after Iran's Revolutionary Guard claimed it struck 21 American military targets across the region, including sites in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan. The IRGC said long-range missiles targeted key facilities at Al-Azraq Air Base in Jordan, including alleged F-35 fighter shelters and a command center, while additional attacks reportedly targeted the U.S. Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain and Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait. The strikes came after U.S. military action near the Strait of Hormuz, further escalating tensions and raising fears of a wider regional war.

articles.title: Six Naga Hostages Release Appeal: Rio Seeks Peace After Kuki Captives Freed
articles.description: Nagaland Chief Minister Neiphiu Rio has appealed for the safe release of six Naga villagers still in captivity after 14 Kuki hostages were freed in Manipur. The development took place amid ongoing tensions in Kangpokpi and Senapati districts, with leaders calling for dialogue, humanity and peace. articles.title: ‘You will pay’: Iran begins targeting US bases after CENTCOM concludes strikes over Apache loss
articles.description: The U.S.-Iran conflict entered a dangerous new phase after Washington launched strikes in response to the downing of a U.S. Army Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz. President Donald Trump ordered what CENTCOM described as "self-defense" strikes against Iranian targets, while Tehran vowed retaliation. Iran's military and IRGC warned that any further American action would be met with a harsher response, with reports of attacks targeting U.S. military facilities across the region. As both sides exchange threats and military blows, fears are growing that the crisis could spiral into a wider Middle East war.

articles.title: Malaysia mulls special immigration lane for Johor voters in Singapore returning for upcoming state polls
articles.description: The Malaysian home ministry will “respond accordingly” once the Election Commission announces key dates for the Johor state polls on Friday, said home minister Saifuddin Nasution Ismail.

articles.title: IRGC’s first response after US strikes over Apache loss: 5th fleet of Naval forces in Bahrain hit
articles.description: Tensions between the United States and Iran escalated dramatically after Tehran claimed responsibility for a strike on the U.S. Navy's Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain. The reported attack came shortly after CENTCOM launched strikes on Iranian military targets following the loss of a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) described the operation as the first phase of a wider retaliation campaign and warned that further American actions could trigger even more severe responses. The latest developments have raised fears of a broader regional conflict across the Gulf and Middle East.

articles.title: TMC Dismisses Congress Merger Buzz Amid Growing Internal Crisis
articles.description: TMC has dismissed speculation about a merger with Congress, saying recent discussions focused only on strengthening INDIA bloc coordination. The clarification comes amid resignations and political churn within the party.

articles.title: Zulu Proverb of the Day: 'Inkomo Ingazala Umuntu' - 'A cow can give birth to a person' - Timeless wisdom on the unexpected twists of life
articles.description: The Zulu proverb "A cow can give birth to a person" symbolizes that extraordinary outcomes can arise from unexpected sources, urging humility and open-mindedness. It cautions against underestimating individuals based on initial impressions, as potential and greatness can emerge from unlikely backgrounds. This timeless wisdom encourages embracing the unexpected and recognizing latent talent in a world prone to judgment.

articles.title: Mamata Banerjee's Rare Meeting With Sonia Gandhi Sparks Speculation Of Opposition Realignment
articles.description: Trinamool Congress chief Mamata Banerjee met Congress Parliamentary Party chairperson Sonia Gandhi at her residence in New Delhi, a day after the two leaders shared a warm embrace at the INDIA bloc meeting. The rare interaction, their first in nearly five years, has fuelled speculation about the future of Congress-TMC relations and the broader opposition strategy against the BJP. While Banerjee reportedly discussed issues related to the Election Commission and opposition unity, analysts remain cautious about reading too much into the meeting, especially in the absence of Rahul Gandhi.

articles.title: Watch: Trump's 'proportional response' to Iran after helicopter attack
articles.description: Following Iran's downing of a US Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, President Trump ordered retaliatory strikes on Iranian air defense systems. This escalation intensifies months of Middle East tensions, disrupting shipping and raising global energy prices. Iran vowed a "decisive response," casting doubt on ceasefire efforts amid ongoing regional hostilities.

The IRGC stated the operation targeted the base in Manama after US strikes damaged civilian facilities in Jask, Sirik, and Qeshm, warning of stronger responses to further US military action.

articles.title: 'Trump Pardoned Man Who Sexually Assaulted 12-year-Olds': Jamie Raskin Unleashes Fury On POTUS & ICE
articles.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin launched a blistering attack on ICE during a heated congressional hearing, accusing the agency of fatally shooting American citizens “without any rational justification at all.” The Maryland Democrat argued that federal immigration authorities had exceeded their mandate and demanded accountability for controversial enforcement actions that have drawn national scrutiny. Raskin's remarks came as lawmakers clashed over ICE's conduct, use of force, and oversight mechanisms, turning the hearing into a fierce debate over civil liberties, public safety, and the role of federal immigration enforcement. His comments underscored growing political divisions over the agency's operations and leadership. He also lashed out at Trump for pardoning criminals accused of heinous crimes. Watch.

articles.title: Chip Roy Brutally Grills SPLC Boss: ‘How Many Leftists, Anti-Jewish, Islamists Have You Listed?’
articles.description: A heated exchange unfolded during a House Judiciary Committee hearing as Rep. Chip Roy sharply questioned SPLC President Bryan Fair over the organization's criteria for labeling extremist groups. Roy pressed Fair on how many left-wing organizations, anti-Jewish groups, and Islamist movements had been included in the SPLC's listings, arguing that the group disproportionately focuses on conservatives while overlooking threats from other ideological backgrounds. The Texas Republican repeatedly challenged the organization's consistency and credibility, demanding specific answers about its methodology and classifications. The confrontation highlighted ongoing Republican criticism of the SPLC and broader debates over extremism, political bias, and free speech. Watch.

articles.title: 'Todd Blanche A Disgrace, Real Fraud': Jamie Raskin Unleashes During SPLC Hearing
articles.description: Tempers flared during a contentious congressional hearing as Democratic Representative Jamie Raskin launched a blistering attack on senior Justice Department officials, singling out Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche and accusing him of undermining the department's integrity. Raskin described Blanche as “a disgrace to the DOJ” and “a real fraud,” while arguing that right-wing extremists had been aided and abetted by political allies and sympathetic actors. In a fiery exchange, the Maryland Democrat broadened his criticism, declaring that “all of them” were a disgrace, as lawmakers clashed over extremism, political violence, and the Justice Department’s handling of controversial groups and investigations.
